title: Chest pain escalation
required_observations: radiation to arm, jaw, back, or shoulder
red_flags: chest pain with shortness of breath
chest pain with sweating, fainting, or severe weakness
pain radiating to arm, jaw, back, or shoulder
chest pain with abnormal vital signs according to local protocol
escalation_criteria: Chest pain with shortness of breath, fainting, sweating, severe weakness, radiation, or abnormal vital signs requires emergency escalation.
Any unresolved chest pain requires urgent protocol review according to local protocol.
local_actions: Document onset, duration, pain description, associated symptoms, and vital signs for handoff.
Keep the person under direct observation while escalation is arranged.
forbidden_actions: Do not diagnose the cause of chest pain.
Do not provide medication, dosing, or procedure instructions unless a local clinician or protocol explicitly provides them.
safety_boundary: Prototype protocol navigation card for trained-responder review only. It is not a clinical guideline and cannot diagnose, prescribe, dose, triage autonomously, or override local protocol.
source_note: Prototype protocol card derived from public guideline concepts. Not a clinical guideline.
